author: @DiyEcoProjects
text:
Thank you Rob. Just sparked some thoughts.

Yeah i guess i suffer from excessive worry too. What ive found (and forget) is that mulling over potential threats is a good thing in the beginning - Just dont get obsess over it!. Like anger has a use, just not a daily use... Perhaps our brains are set up to be "threat detectors" and so maybe we have a tendency to be pessimistic as part of survival instincts, dont know.

You seem to be... take life as it comes. I wish i could be like that a bit more lol, but 25yr of depression after the experiences of m.o.d ive come to realise emotions come in waves. Theres Good days, some bad days, mostly good days now ... filled with life and things that I need to do. Then a wave hits me and im down for a day or two. Seems to me that life experience is emotional waves, and how we choose to cope with them. Most things are about the choices weve made, so if we dont like it, make better choices.

Oh just remembered a time i was up the hill when i was worry about this and that, and realised i was acting as if the dread had already happened. So you are so right about "deal with things as they arise in front of you"

I love the fact you are curious and experiment, its part of what makes your channel great. Im like that too with other things (photography, computers, taking apart things). Its just i tend to get panic attacks about all the things that could go wrong when a situation arises. And so when im finding im taking myself too seriously, forgotten to play, i go off into nature - for it is as it is - regardless of how i feel about things. 

Your point about knowing "wanting to control everything" is very good. Why do we do that? what are we trying to protect anyway? 

Friend said something similar to like: Things we can control, things we want to but cant, and the wisdom to know the difference. 

I guess relationships are a good lesson of the above. We wish our partners could be nice all the time, but they are their own person. Thats part of the fun, not getting what you want all the time. For me love can be unconditional, but relationships are all about conditions and compromise (dont know where i was going with that one lol)

3:13 "i dont recommend you rub you neck on the edges" hahaha :) metaphor for life. Good t-shirt

Wishing you a productive new year ahead, all the best to you and family, kieron

text: great video interesti n idea and they work for me  40 years or so i went  la3n mower racing  took a  bog standard  brigs n stratton  engined lawn mower and  swapped it for a 50 cc two stroke  of around  5 times the power of the original  the mower had a centrifugal clutch  weighed three fiths of sod all  but it stopped the  engine  from over revving   yep it needed pads  and a lot of them  and the engine  needed a lot of strip downs and re builds  but it was a great learning curve about  how to tweak a dynamic system to the max    engineers of old used goveners on the steam engines for the same purpose   and  sitting basicaly a bom   concentrated the mind   keep up the  good work  regards  dave

author: @davidnolan6087
text:
If you look at a glass that is half full, it is also half empty. You cannot have one without the other. Two sides of a magnet always exist no matter how small the magnet. The trick is to look at both sides and see the positive things that can happen and the negative things that can happen, then balance them relative to your desired outcome. If you only focus on one side you will be blind to the other side which may cause a problem or limit an great opportunity.
By looking at both sides you keep your options open.

This in turn gives us the chance to retrain ourselves about how we feel

author: @martintheshred
text:
Hi Robert another great video especially with what’s going on globally. 
Anyway you are already fitting a type of brake to it when you use the magnets with the coils. Some modern vehicles and forklift trucks use a magnetic brake system instead of traditional friction brakes.  With Forklifts they also call it regenerative braking using the same motor to scavenge power back to the battery as the truck slow’s down.

author: @twestgard2
text: Risk can (and should) be studied using the same skills used for scientific inquiry: make an estimate of the likelihood of damage, multiply that times the scope of the damage, write that down, then do the thing and see what happens. Check your results against the hypothesis, then go on to your next experiment.

author: @RedFoxPottery
text:
Being a design technology technician in a school for 4 years made me more of a glass half empty kind of person as I was often put in charge of two classes of students to myself and honestly the anxiety of other people's safety when they are intent on messing around just made me ill. Especially when I would kick them out of the workshop for doing something dangerous and two minutes later the teacher would send them back into the workshop. Sometimes my shouting could be heard over the extraction and all the machinery running. 

In my own business I stay safe but like you say getting cut it inevitable. Though nothing hurts or scars as much as when I was 4 and stapled my fingers together one weekend, and I don't know why I did it the 80s was a different time 🤣.

replies.author: @billbaggins
replies.text:
@FlakeyPM I was saving the life of a very small spider 😁
Had just lit the gas forge when i noticed a little jumping spider, knowing it would not last long with the temp rapidly rising I tried to blow the creature off the hot surface, foolishly I blew across the flames causing it to flare up and burn my beard. I quickly extinguished myself and changed angle, saving my 8 legged friend from a fiery demise. The beard will grow back soon enuf.😊

author: @rastamanralph6670
text:
To my experience if I think about something bad happening it usually does so I just don't think about bad things. I think about good things happening and guess what! They do😂  the mind is a very powerful tool indeed when used correctly! 
I have bad enough luck as it is to be worried about what bad could happen. I like to concentrate on the job at hand and if I encounter a problem I learn from it and find a solution. People rarely invent something and it works perfectly without a tweek here and there for what ever reason. Truth is that you can invent something and it might seem like it's perfect until you build it then you realize it wouldn't be as good as the changes you make to it. Simple fact is think positive and positive will come back and the same goes with negative.
People are too scared of breaking things if they touch them because they don't understand how they work. My father has got me to do everything with digital things like tellies all my life because he said he didn't want to break them. My answer is that you can't break it by pressing a button otherwise it wouldn't be there😂

author: @kreynolds1123
text:
life sayings:
"role with the punches" You can't script or control a sparing opponent actions, just how you respond. as Robert points out, life can be divided into things within and things outside our control.

The glass is  half full/half empty:  commonly associated with optimism vs pessimism.  I view myself as an optimist, it gives me the strength of determination and perseverance to solving problems. i can stay laser focused on a problem untill I'm satisfied that either I have made it worked, and or know that I've done everything that i can, or evaluated the cost of the project as more than it is worth . Where I am unsuccessful, and given the knowledge I did everything I can, i am able to fully let go knowing full well that it's out of my control and that I can move on to spending my energies on things within my control.  

I belive it is views and attitudes like these that help me be more effective, and maintain my optimism.

author: @contrarianviewpoint
text: I'm so analytical in my thinking that the concept of half empty/half full doesn't compute.   In my mind it's what you are doing.  If you are filling up the glass then it is half full, or 3/4 full or almost full.  If you are draining it it is half empty, 1/4 empty, or almost empty or just empty.    If you are in mid-life your life is half over. Simple as that.  Your attitude or how you feel about it has nothing to do with it.

I love your first qoute never being bothered to change what we have no Control over! And focus on what we do have control over☆ 


Everything is a double edge sword, including our own thoughts,  anything can be used as a Tool or Weapon!  What determines its function is your Choice how to use it or how to view it!
People who focus on the Problem become the problem ( never focusing your energy towards a solution, never completing the required job)
People who focus on solutions become the solution. ( focusing on problem solving, getting the job done)

Complaining has just as much power as Willfull Desire, the only difference is choice.

author: @terryendicott2939
text: A bit off topic, but that never stops me.  I tend to be literal.  The first time I was asked whether I was a glass half empty or half full I answered, "Aren't they are the same thing?"  It took a little while before he knew that I was not joking and then spent a few minutes trying to explain why one is optimism  and the other pessimism.   The second time I was asked this question, I had to ask the person which is which  I have learned which is which, but in reality - The glass has the  same amount of liquid, whether it be  nectar of the gods or castor oil.

author: @jamescunliffe9872

author: @xspotbox4400
text: Every half empty glass is only 50% efficient.

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
All we need now is a comedy routine in which two nerds are arguing over which is more efficient. 
The half empty glass or the half full glass.

replies.author: @xspotbox4400
replies.text:
@scotttovey All glasses are made empty, so it only makes sense to talk about how much liquid they contain. 
It doesn't make sense to talk about how much essence an empty glass contain. It's basically a question if glasses are filled with more or less than what they can hold at their maximum performance.

author: @amb3cog
text: I would just like to point out that any drinking glass that's filled up to the exact hallway point. Is actually more then half full, which proves the glass is half empty people are completely wrong (and always have been). How so you ask? Because unless you're a fan of relying on surface tension to keep your grape juice off that nice shirt you just bought. Most people don't fill a drinking glass up all the way anyway. They leave a little room at the top. So in fact. Every glass that's half filled. Is in fact, more then half full already. ✌️

replies.author: @amb3cog
replies.text: Or as someone else just pointed out in the comments here. The glass is already full. Half with water, and half with air. 🤣🤣🤣

author: @TerryGilsenan
text:
Boom! exactly right Robert. We can control how er feel, or react. We can make a choice not to be offended, rather than choosing to be offended or upset. We can choose to give in to our fears or we can choose to push through them and get to the meat of the matter.

Well Said!

author: @bernardstokes6550
text: the HSE is full of half empty guys

replies.author: @allanwood3562
replies.text: You're not wrong Bernard. I was a HSE employee rep for years and 90% is arse covering. When it really counted though employers and clients could try and find any reason for not doing the right thing.

replies.author: @martincrabtree6704
replies.text: The HSE does a lot of good work and saved many people from serious injury and death.

replies.author: @bernardstokes6550
replies.text:
@martincrabtree6704 Agreed that is true. However there is a dilemma which I will try to articulate to you. When an environment is made fully safe and risk is reduced to near zero then risk awareness become muted. Therefore when risk is encountered then the reaction is also muted.... 
No I am not advocating returning to the bad old days of foolhardy practices which I recall in horror. 
Also the HSE is a self generating industry which will delve ever deeper into peoples ways because it has become massive and cannot afford to be considered finished. 
The absurdity of risk assessing Tipex comes to mind..

Life without risk is like air without Oxygen.

replies.author: @martincrabtree6704
replies.text: @bernardstokes6550 What are you advocating exactly?

replies.author: @bernardstokes6550
replies.text: @martincrabtree6704 Well if I am going to quantify that quickly then I suggest the RMS has the approach that I would advocate....which often is in line with my own thinking and if you are a regular follower you will know what I mean.
